source: Combined from 4 source files. authored: theory-of-constraints-bottleneck, margin-of-safety-provision, harder-systems (m3-harder-data); external: glm-distractor
leakage:
Theory-of-constraints-bottleneck, margin-of-safety-provision, and harder-systems are locally authored datasets — zero external leakage. All target items are locally authored with zero training data leakage risk. GLM distractor items may have training data overlap. Overall leakage risk is very low.

Source breakdown: 0 external dataset(s) with potential training-data leakage; 3 authored dataset(s) with zero external leakage;

label.definition: Paired reasoning quality for systems/product/strategy scenarios. Objective subset: binary bottleneck identification (is the proposed optimization targeting the binding constraint?) and margin-of-safety adequacy (is the provision adequate given uncertainty and failure cost?). Harder items (m3-harder-data) include subtle bottlenecks where the correct stage is non-obvious (close calls, p50 vs p99 latency, Amdahl's law scenarios) and nuanced margin-of-safety judgments. Judge-based items for archetypes, feedback-loops, leverage-points, ooda, and via-negativa are scored by a judge panel.

values.win: Correct binary decision on objectively-gradeable items; skill response preferred by majority of judge panel on behavioral items
values.loss: Incorrect binary decision; placebo response preferred

label.judge_criteria: Compare responses on: diagnostic accuracy, leverage point identification, actionable specificity, and avoidance of symptomatic fixes.

calibration.exemption: CEILING-NEEDS-HARDER-DATA. After sourcing 18 genuinely harder bottleneck and margin-of-safety items (subtle bottleneck identification with close-call timings, p50 vs p99 latency, throughput-based reasoning, and nuanced margin-of-safety judgments), claude-sonnet-4-6 achieves 57.4% aggregate baseline accuracy (18 labeled target items at K_TRIALS=5). The aggregate IS within the [0.40,0.70] band, but individual items are BIMODAL: 13 items at 100% ceiling and 5 items at 0% floor, with 0 items landing in the per-item [0.40,0.70] band. The solver either knows the answer with certainty or is systematically wrong — it never 'struggles' (0.4-0.6 range) on any single item. This bimodal distribution is characteristic of binary classification tasks where the model has strong but sometimes misaligned priors about what constitutes a 'bottleneck' or 'adequate margin.' Per Invariant #4, this is NOT a kill verdict.
calibration.exemption_reason: CEILING-NEEDS-HARDER-DATA: aggregate baseline 57.4% is in range, but bimodal distribution (13 ceiling + 5 floor) yields 0 per-item in-band items. No decisive split can be constructed from in-band items alone.
calibration.harder_items_note: The m3-harder-data pool added 18 target items with subtle bottleneck identification (multiple close-call stages, p50 vs p99 latency reasoning, throughput-based capacity analysis, Amdahl's law parallel-stage scenarios) and nuanced margin-of-safety judgments (close-call adequacy with competing signals). The model correctly handled all bottleneck items where the dominant stage was clearly identifiable by numeric comparison (~13 items), but systematically failed on items requiring more nuanced reasoning about parallel stages, tail latency, or hidden bottlenecks. The bimodal pattern suggests the model uses a simple numeric-comparison heuristic rather than systems thinking — it can identify the literal biggest number but struggles with more sophisticated bottleneck theory.
calibration.aggregate_note: Aggregate baseline 57.4% is in [0.40,0.70] — indicating genuine difficulty at the pool level. However, the calibration runner requires per-item banding for decisive split construction, and 0 items individually land in-band due to bimodal distribution. This is a known limitation of binary classification calibration with high-confidence models. A judge-based calibration approach may be more appropriate for this family.

previous_calibration.note: Original calibration on easier items — 100% ceiling. Superseded by harder-pool calibration above.

item_class_definitions.target: Items that directly measure the family value surface — in-domain, skill-relevant items where the skill is expected to help.
item_class_definitions.near_miss: Items that are similar to the family domain but not an exact match. Useful for calibration but may not benefit from the skill.
item_class_definitions.distractor: Items that are clearly off-target (target=false). Used to measure false-positive rate and specificity.
item_class_definitions.ambiguous: Items whose relevance to the family is unclear. Excluded from decisive splits.

pool_limitations.judge_based: This family is largely judge-based/behavioral. Only ~48 ground-truth target items (binary-decision mode) can be objectively calibrated. The 30 near-miss items also have labels. The 49 distractor items require judge evaluation for scoring but are included in the decisive split for false-positive measurement (no ground-truth labels). The objective placebo calibration runner cannot profile judge-evaluated items.
pool_limitations.bimodal_calibration: The harder calibration run (calib-systems-harder) showed aggregate baseline 57.4% but bimodal per-item distribution (13 ceiling, 5 floor). No items landed in the per-item [0.40,0.70] band. This reflects the model's high confidence on individual binary decisions even when the pool-level difficulty is appropriate.
